plan: In SQLCompiler.get_group_by (django/db/models/sql/compiler.py, the order_by loop around line 128), stop adding Random() to the GROUP BY: when iterating non-ref order_by expressions, filter out Random instances before extending the group-by expressions (e.g. expressions.extend(exp for exp in expr.get_group_by_cols() if not isinstance(exp, Random)); Random is already imported at the top of compiler.py). Alternatively/additionally, django/db/models/functions/math.py's Random could override get_group_by_cols to return [] since a random function is not a meaningful grouping key. Add a regression test asserting that annotate(Count(...)).order_by('?') keeps the aggregation grouped only by the model's columns.
